question_text: Let {0.90, 0.50, 0.01, 0.95} be a realization of a random sample of size 4 from the probability density function $ f ( x ) = { \left\{ \begin{array} { l l } { { \frac { \theta } { ( 1 - \theta ) } } x ^ { ( 2 \theta - 1 ) / ( 1 - \theta ) } , } & { 0 < x < 1 , } \\ { 0 , } & { { \mathrm { o t h e r w i s e } } , } \end{array} \right. } $ where $0 . 5 \leq \theta < 1$ . Then the maximum likelihood estimate of θ based on the observed sample equals (round off to 2 decimal places).
solution:
The likelihood function is constructed using the given probability density function. Taking the log-likelihood and maximizing it, we find the maximum likelihood estimate of $\theta \colon$

 $ \begin{array} { r } { \hat { \theta } \approx 0 . 5 0 . } \end{array} $ 

Thus, the value is 0.50.
Quick Tip

To estimate parameters using the maximum likelihood method, construct the likelihood function and maximize it with respect to the parameter of interest.
